assignments.value: { "title 1": "Act as a Pinterest SEO expert. Write under 90 characters a clickable title  for a pin about [Keyword]. Include high-volume keywords Choose the hook that is most contextually relevant to the Pin's topic (e.g., use \"For Small Spaces\" only for decor/organization topics, use \"Under $20\" for budget topics). Ensure variety: Do not repeat the same hook multiple times if generating a batch of titles. Use the full range of the list. The structure must be: [Hook Phrase]: [Main Keyword/Title] OR [Hook Phrase] [Main Keyword/Title]. Hook Phrases List: For Moms, For Beginners, For Renters, For Small Spaces, For Busy Women, For Teens, For Families, Mistakes, Avoid, Stop Doing This, Nobody Tells You, Warning, Don’t Do This, Cheap, On a Budget, Under $20, Affordable, Low-Cost, Save Money, Lazy, Effortless, 5-Minute, Stress-Free, Simplest, Done-for-You, Step-by-Step, How To, Fast, In Minutes, No-Skill, Zero Effort, Do This, Must-See, Game-Changer, Aesthetic, Beautiful, Gorgeous, Dreamy, Modern, Minimalist, Chic, Cute, Quick, Cozy, Genius, Stunning, Secret, Cheap, Budget, Small, Beginner. Selection Rules: Rules were set to ensure that the AI does not choose randomly, but selects what fits the topic (for example it won't use the word \"Delicious\" for a décor topic, and similarly, it won't use \"For Renters\" for a cooking recipe). Ensure variety: I instructed it to vary the choices over time to cover the entire list and not focus on a single word like \"How To.\" Structure: I specified the sentence structure to ensure that the word appears at the beginning of the title.
